definition: A rule for representing information in computational form.
Conversion of structured data into a storable or transferable form.
A method for reducing representation size.
Compression that discards information to reduce size.
Compressed size divided by original size.
A method that stores repeated runs compactly.
A method assigning shorter codes to more frequent symbols.
A method replacing repeated strings with references.
A compact value used to detect accidental change.
A rule mapping characters to code points or bytes.
A standard for representing text across writing systems.
A structural rule set for fields
Ability for systems to exchange and interpret data consistently.
Context needed to interpret encoded or compressed information.
Risk that encoding or compression removes
